geological/mining experts from Paris and Berlin
Hedwig (court girl, later General Schwartz's wife)

murder victim under bandstand (unnamed man with gold pin)

Rev. J. C. Elman (Vicar of Little Purlington)
Blackmail by Arthur Carstairs (of his own family in disguise)
Attempted assassination of Father Brown by hotel proprietor
Attempted Murder of Sir Henry Baskerville
Attempted murder of Walter Pendragon (drowned when caught)
Murder disguised as burglary (John Bankes kills Moonshine, steals gems)
Battle of the Black River / sacrifice of eight hundred soldiers
Perjury of John Boulnois (lying to American journalist)
Suicide of Sir Claude Champion (self-inflicted sword wound)
Theft of Roman coin by Christabel (minor)
Death of Sir Aaron Armstrong (suicide/accidental killing)
Theft of Mrs. Bankes's emerald necklace
Historical crimes of Eyre family (wife scourging, church fire, etc.)

Financial fraud / usurpation of dukedom by Isaac Green

Murder of man under bandstand (with gold monkey pin)

Murder by Parkinson (with stage spear) and subsequent death from grief

Sabotage of fountain pen (by Joan Stacey)

Execution of General St. Clare by his own men
Treason of General St. Clare (selling secrets to Brazil)
Staged burglary (silver theft as blind for poison plot)
The staged duel (Maurice falls as if shot, then shoots James)
Sutton Mystery (strangled man at Epsom, referenced)

Theft of the silver fish service

Treason (during the war, by the Captain)

brown-paper parcels (modern English coins hidden in)

cruet-stand (hidden in dustbin, with emetic contents)

flat shining dagger (dropped by hotel proprietor)
secret note / forged paper (noiseless powder formula direction)
Giant gilt eye (symbol of Apollo cult)
gold pin with monkey head (murder weapon)

iron garden seat (used by Flambeau as weapon)

Oriental dagger (crooked knife inlaid with stones)
Oriental musical instrument (silver/steel, like crooked violin)
Prince Otto's military sash / scarf (used as gag/murder weapon)
Pantomime scenery (Blue Palace gates, Enchanted Grove)
Pendragon's map (of river, mistaken for Pacific chart)
portrait of Paul Arnhold (Chamberlain, white hair, black eyebrows)

razor (used to cut Cray's throat - first incident)

Short cloak/cape with silver clasp and chain
Silver model of St. Clare's tomb (in the inn)
Diary of an English soldier (found by Brazilians)
South Sea idols (two stone lumps at entrance)
steel inner cabin / safe (Prince Otto's room)
Temple of the Monkey (idol / Monkey's Tail)
Threatening letters (red ink on stamp paper)
three-cornered cicatrice / scar (on Green's bald head)
